danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary or moving beam hazards in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat that activates/deactivates based on gravity orientation; avoid or time movement

danger_signals.visual_form: Heavy mechanical blocks that compress when player is in range
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal environmental hazard; gravity flip required to escape or bypass
Direct combat threat; requires spatial awareness and gravity switching to dodge

danger_signals.visual_form: Narrow passages that loop horizontally; exit one side to reappear opposite
danger_signals.meaning: Disorienting navigation hazard combined with gravity mechanics; tests concentration and spatial reasoning

danger_signals.visual_form: Robotic laser cannon at Pluto base (inferred from plot context)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger: the station's self-destruct system is failing; player must manually intervene

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary offensive capability; limited ammunition, emphasizes positioning over firepower
Temporary invulnerability or damage reduction; allows brief safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Special power-up that activates during gravity flip
safety_signals.meaning: Invincibility frames during gravity transition; provides momentary protection and offensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Floor or ceiling geometry that does not have active hazards
safety_signals.meaning: Momentary rest point; safe to reorient before next hazard

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pluto defense outpost; characterized as 'most sophisticated combat mech available'

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Automated robotic cannon system on Pluto base
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Self-destruct system failure; cannon has turned against creators; destroyed Neptune, targeting Earth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pluto base interior with active hazards (lasers, crushers); environmental threats are expressions of the cannon's failing systems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hazards activate/deactivate unpredictably; enemy patterns reflect chaotic AI behavior; bosses escalate difficulty reflecting weapon system degradation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite flips orientation; visual perspective inverts; hazards that were on ceiling become floor-based threats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy projectile patterns shift; platform-jumping becomes ceiling-jumping; layout interpretation changes; same physical space becomes strategically different

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Penetrating deeper into Pluto facility; visual design escalates technical complexity; parallax scrolling intensifies to show depth
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy and hazard complexity increases; boss encounters become more elaborate; pattern recognition demands grow; Expert Mode completely reconfigures all elements
Earth leaders reward M-308 Gunner with immortality and new mission: perpetual protector of mankind against future threats

environmental_storytelling.example: Laser emitters and crushing blocks are visual manifestations of the malfunctioning cannon system; their chaotic activation patterns tell the story of a rogue AI without cutscenes
Moving from outer Pluto base toward the cannon core; environmental complexity increases, visually communicating deeper infiltration and escalating danger
Multiple background layers scrolling at different speeds create sense of vast facility interior; technical achievement reinforces sci-fi setting and scale of the installation
Constant orientation reversals mirror the disorienting effect of a rogue automated system; gameplay communicates the alien, mechanical nature of the threat

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss encounters as cannon subsystem confrontations
environmental_storytelling.example: Final stage boss rush represents encounters with different weapon systems or defensive mechanisms; visual variety of bosses tells of a multi-layered installation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves horizontally on floor; jumps upward; fires Power Beam; uses gravity flip to reverse polarity
Moves horizontally on ceiling; jumps 'downward' (toward floor); weapon trajectory inverts; environmental threats appear from different angles
Fire projectiles in patterns designed around gravity states; attack patterns change based on player's current orientation; position themselves relative to hazards
Predictable pattern recognition; learnable attack sequences; defeat grants stage completion
Completely new attack patterns; increased complexity; removal of most power-ups; demands perfect memorization and execution; mirrors arcade difficulty
Activate/deactivate based on gravity orientation; player must predict which surface is safe; timing and positioning are critical
Exiting left side causes reappearance on right; spatial continuity breaks; combined with gravity reversal, creates disorientation effect

notes: Metal Storm uses its core gravity-reversal mechanic as both gameplay and narrative device. The world-state of the malfunctioning cannon is expressed through environmental hazards that activate unpredictably, mirroring AI failure. Danger signals are almost entirely environmental (lasers, crushers, projectiles) rather than faction-based, reflecting a mission against a facility rather than against organized enemies. The two-faction structure (Humanity vs. Rogue AI) is implicit in narrative rather than visually distinguished in-game. The parallax scrolling technical achievement serves narrative purpose by emphasizing the vast, deep nature of the Pluto facility. Expert Mode functions as a world-state change where difficulty systems are completely reconfigured, reflecting hypothetical escalation of threat. Safety signals are minimal (power-ups only), forcing reliance on movement skill and spatial mastery. The game communicates state primarily through mechanical and environmental changes rather than color coding or symbolic markers.
